history.historicalImportance: Banka has a rich historical and cultural heritage and was historically part of the ancient Anga Mahajanapada. The district is also known for the Mandar Hill, an important site in Hindu and Jain traditions.
history.formationHistory: Banka district was carved out of the erstwhile Bhagalpur district and officially came into existence on 21 February 1991.
history.freedomMovement: The people of Banka actively participated in India's freedom movement through the Swadeshi Movement, Non-Cooperation Movement and Quit India Movement.
history.archaeologicalImportance: The district contains several ancient temples, inscriptions and archaeological remains, especially around Mandar Hill.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period - Part of the Anga Mahajanapada
Mauryan period - Came under the Maurya Empire
Gupta period - Flourished under the Gupta Empire
Medieval period - Ruled by the Delhi Sultanate and Mughal Empire
1765 - Came under British administration after the Treaty of Allahabad
21 February 1991 - Banka became a separate district

geography.location: Banka district is located in the south-eastern part of Bihar and shares its southern boundary with Jharkhand.
geography.climate: Subtropical climate with hot summers, monsoon rains and cool winters
geography.forests: Moderate forest cover, mainly in the southern and south-eastern parts

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of rural income in Banka district.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is practiced in ponds, reservoirs and other water bodies across the district.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Banka district is connected by national highways, state highways and a network of major district and rural roads.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Deoghar Airport (Jharkhand). Jay Prakash Narayan International Airport, Patna is another major airport serving the district.
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport Corporation (BSRTC), private buses, taxis and auto-rickshaws provide transport services.
infrastructure.electricity: Most villages and urban areas have electricity connectivity under the Bihar power distribution network.
infrastructure.waterSupply: Urban areas receive piped water supply, while rural areas depend on hand pumps, wells and government water supply schemes.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G and 5G mobile networks are available in most parts of the district, with broadband services in urban areas.

education.governmentSchools: More than 1,700 government primary, middle and secondary schools operate across the district.
education.privateSchools: Several CBSE, ICSE and Bihar Board affiliated private schools are located in Banka district.
universities: No university is located in Banka district. Colleges are affiliated with Mandar Hill University, Bounsi.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district

healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres (PHCs) operate across all blocks of the district.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta, while women wear Saree and Salwar-Kameez.
historicalHeritage: Ancient Jain and Hindu heritage sites
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Banka
Department of Art, Culture and Youth, Bihar

wildlifeSanctuaries: No wildlife sanctuary within the district

sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in state-level competitions
School and college teams represent Banka in Bihar-level tournaments

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector and private sector banks are available across Banka district.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking, AEPS and digital payment services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: The district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM), Banka.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Banka Police under the Bhagalpur Police Range.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Banka, Amarpur and other major towns to provide emergency fire services.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through the Public Distribution System (PDS) across the district.
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.policeDistrict: Banka Police District under Bhagalpur Police Range
lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulates traffic and road safety across Banka district.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Banka coordinates disaster preparedness, flood management, drought response and emergency relief operations.

environment.forestCover: Moderate forest cover, mainly in the southern hilly region of Banka district.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post and parcel services across Banka district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G and 5G mobile connectivity is available in most parts of Banka district.

stateRankings: One of the major forest-covered districts of Bihar
Known for Mandar Hill and religious tourism
Important producer of silk, lac and agricultural products
nationalRankings: Mandar Hill is a nationally important pilgrimage and heritage site
Known for eco-tourism and historical significance
notableAchievements: Mandar Hill is associated with the Samudra Manthan legend
Major producer of silk and lac in Bihar
developmentIndicators: Growth in healthcare and education facilities
Promotion of eco-tourism and rural tourism
records: Mandar Hill is one of the most important historical and religious landmarks of Bihar
Banka is among Bihar's most forest-rich districts

famousPersonalities: Jaya Prakash Narayan (associated with the region)
Prominent Saints associated with Mandar Hill
importantDates: 21 February 1991 – Banka became a separate district
15 August 1947 – Became part of independent India
additionalInformation: Mandar Hill is one of the most important religious and historical sites of Bihar.
Agriculture remains the primary occupation of the district.
The district is known for silk, lac production and forest resources.
Banka shares its southern boundary with Jharkhand.
